nodes.context: Italian American Mafia crime family based in New Orleans, Louisiana, active since late 19th century.
Early American Mafia crime family in New Orleans, established by Charles and Antonio Matranga, active late 19th century until Prohibition.
Sicilian family in New Orleans involved in fruit loading operations, rival of Matranga crime family.
Organized crime group based in Chicago, led by Al Capone in the 1920s-30s.
New York Mafia family led by Frank Costello, associated with Carlos Marcello.
Criminal organization operating in the Southern United States, allied with Marcello's New Orleans family.
Motorcycle gang used by Marcello for debt collection and contract killing.
Mafia family based in Buffalo, New York, listed as ally in infobox.
Mafia family based in Cleveland, Ohio, listed as ally in infobox.
Mafia family based in Dallas, Texas, listed as ally in infobox.
New York Mafia family, listed as ally in infobox.

Mafia family based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, listed as ally in infobox.

Italian American Mafia crime family based in New Orleans, Louisiana, involved in racketeering, extortion, gambling, etc.
New York-based Mafia family involved in video poker and other rackets in New Orleans.
Philadelphia-based Mafia family that sought to move into casino gambling and cocaine trafficking in New Orleans.
New York-based Mafia family involved in video poker infiltration in Louisiana.
Motorcycle club whose Tampa chapter carried out a car bomb killing ordered by a Genovese family associate.
Italian American Mafia crime family based in New Orleans, Louisiana, involved in racketeering, extortion, gambling, etc.
Italian American Mafia crime family, one of the Five Families in New York.

edges.evidence_quote: The Matranga crime family, established by Charles (1857 – October 28, 1943) and Antonio (Tony) Matranga (d. 1890), was one of the earliest recorded American Mafia crime families, operating in New Orleans during the late 19th century until the beginning of Prohibition in 1920.
edges.context: The Matranga family was the predecessor of the New Orleans crime family; Carollo succeeded Matranga.

edges.evidence_quote: They eventually began moving in on Provenzano fruit loading operations intimidating them with threats of violence.
edges.context: Matranga brothers extorted and intimidated the Provenzano family, leading to a gang war.

edges.evidence_quote: Al Capone supposedly traveled to the city demanding Carollo supply the Chicago Outfit (rather than Chicago's Sicilian Mafia boss Joe Aiello) with imported alcohol.
edges.context: Carollo refused to supply the Chicago Outfit, leading to a confrontation where Carollo disarmed Capone's bodyguards.

edges.evidence_quote: This ordeal got him involved with Frank Costello, leader of the Genovese crime family in New York City, where he began working for Costello.
edges.context: Carlos Marcello began working for Frank Costello of the Genovese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The New Orleans family was also closely linked with the Dixie Mafia, initially through Marcello's association with LeRoy Hobbs, the Sheriff of Harrison County, Mississippi.
edges.context: Marcello permitted the Dixie Mafia to operate in New Orleans in exchange for a percentage of earnings and non-encroachment.

edges.evidence_quote: Under Marcello's rule, the family outsourced debt collection and contract killing to members of the Dixie Mafia and the Outlaws Motorcycle Club.
edges.context: The New Orleans family used the Outlaws MC for debt collection and contract killing.

edges.evidence_quote: Carollo and an associate, Sebastian "Buster" Salvatore, met in New York with John Gotti and Salvatore "Sammy the Bull" Gravano of the Gambino family to seek assistance in taking over the soon-to-be-legalized video poker industry in Louisiana.
edges.context: New Orleans family sought assistance from Gambino family for video poker industry.

edges.evidence_quote: Carollo's underboss, Frank "Fat Frank" Gagliano Sr., also authorized a request by representatives of the Philadelphia family for permission to move into casino gambling and cocaine trafficking in New Orleans.
edges.context: New Orleans family authorized Philadelphia family to operate in New Orleans.

edges.evidence_quote: In May 1994, following an FBI sting dubbed "Operation Hard Crust", Carollo with 16 members of the Marcello, Gambino and Genovese families were arrested on charges of infiltrating the newly legalized Louisiana video poker industry.
edges.context: Members of New Orleans and Genovese families were arrested together for video poker infiltration.

edges.evidence_quote: The bombing was ordered by Anthony "Hialeah Tony" Scire, a South Florida -based Genovese family associate and cocaine supplier, and carried out by members of the Tampa chapter of the Outlaws Motorcycle Club.
edges.context: Genovese associate ordered a bombing carried out by Outlaws Motorcycle Club.

edges.evidence_quote: Kastel, a New York-born Jewish mobster, was an associate of the Marcello and Genovese families.
edges.context: Phillip Kastel was an associate of both the New Orleans crime family (Marcello) and the Genovese family.
